plan_title: Active Contracts Expiring in Next 10 Days (Email Summary)
plan_description: Reads a Google Sheets-based contract tracker, finds contracts that are still active and expiring within the next 10 days, and emails an HTML summary table to you.

data: - Read the contract tracker from the Google Sheets spreadsheet with spreadsheet_id "1RHLbBXzrKv24gNgp7a4Lr7RlMIrP9fjacfGWjhPAbOE".
- Use the single sheet tab named "contracts" as the source table.
- Treat row 1 as the header row that defines column names.
- Use the column named "Contract Ends Due Date" as the contract expiration/end date field.
- Use the following columns (by header name) as the report fields: "Contract ID", "Company Name", "Contract Amount", "Contract Ends Due Date", "Email of Contact Person".
actions: - Read all rows from the "contracts" tab and map each row into a contract record using the header row as keys.
- Determine whether a contract is still active by checking if "Contract Ends Due Date" is today or later.
- For active contracts only, include the contract in the results if "Contract Ends Due Date" falls within the next 10 days (inclusive).
- Sort the results by "Contract Ends Due Date" ascending (soonest expiring first).
- If there are 0 matching contracts, prepare a "none found" message instead of a table.
output: - If there are matching contracts, generate an HTML table (for embedding in an email body) containing one row per matching contract.
- The HTML table columns must be exactly: Contract ID, Company Name, Contract Amount, Contract Ends Due Date, Email of Contact Person.
- If there are 0 matching contracts, output a short message stating that no active contracts expire in the next 10 days.
delivery: - Email subject must be exactly: "Contracts expiring in the next 10 days".
- If there are matching contracts, embed the HTML table in the email body.
- If there are 0 matching contracts, send the "none found" message in the email body.
processing_steps: - Load the full contracts table from Google Sheets.
- Normalize "Contract Ends Due Date" values into a comparable date format.
- Filter to active contracts (end date today or later).
- Filter active contracts to those expiring within the next 10 days (inclusive).
- Sort results by end date ascending.
- Render either an HTML table (matches) or a plain message (no matches).
- Send the email to the user.

resolved_user_inputs.value: Contract ID, Company Name, Contract Amount, Contract Ends Due Date, Email of Contact Person
Contracts expiring in the next 10 days
